vocabulary.meaning: a distinctive way of pronouncing a language, especially one associated with a particular country, area, or social class
vocabulary.example: She has a strong French accent when she speaks English.

vocabulary.meaning: a formal public statement about a fact, occurrence, or intention
vocabulary.example: The head teacher made an announcement about the school trip.

vocabulary.meaning: to send out programs on TV or radio, or a specific television/radio program
vocabulary.example: The concert will be broadcast live tomorrow night. / I missed the evening news broadcast.

vocabulary.meaning: a television station; or a passage of water
vocabulary.example: What channel is the football match on?

vocabulary.meaning: easy to perceive, understand, or interpret
vocabulary.example: His explanation was very clear and easy to follow.

vocabulary.meaning: to press a button on a computer mouse or device
vocabulary.example: Click on the link below to visit our website.

vocabulary.meaning: to communicate with someone; or communication/relationship
vocabulary.example: You can contact me by email or phone. / We must stay in close contact during the trip.

vocabulary.meaning: a collection of data stored on a computer, or a folder for paper documents
vocabulary.example: Please save the file to your desktop.

vocabulary.meaning: serious and correct, suitable for official or serious situations
vocabulary.example: You need to wear formal clothes to the job interview.

vocabulary.meaning: a picture, photo, or representation of something
vocabulary.example: The website contains many beautiful images of nature.

vocabulary.meaning: relaxed and friendly, suitable for everyday conversations
vocabulary.example: He sent an informal invitation to his friends for a barbecue.

vocabulary.meaning: the global computer network providing information and communication facilities
vocabulary.example: I use the internet to do research for my homework.

vocabulary.meaning: to stop someone from speaking by saying or doing something
vocabulary.example: It is impolite to interrupt when someone else is talking.

vocabulary.meaning: to connect two or more things; or a connection/web address
vocabulary.example: The bridge links the island to the mainland. / Follow the link to read the article.

vocabulary.meaning: the main means of mass communication (broadcasting, publishing, and the internet) regarded collectively
vocabulary.example: The news media reported on the change in weather.

vocabulary.meaning: a portable telephone that can make and receive calls
vocabulary.example: I left my mobile phone at home, so I couldn't call you.

vocabulary.meaning: connected to the internet or happening on the internet
vocabulary.example: Do you prefer shopping online or in stores? / I went online to find the flight schedule.

vocabulary.meaning: to stop doing something for a short time; or a short break in action
vocabulary.example: We paused the movie to get some snacks. / There was a brief pause in the conversation.

vocabulary.meaning: to convince someone to do something through reasoning or argument
vocabulary.example: I managed to persuade my parents to let me go to the camp.

vocabulary.meaning: to make the sound of a word or letter in a particular way
vocabulary.example: How do you pronounce your last name?

vocabulary.meaning: to prepare and print a book, magazine, or other material for sale
vocabulary.example: They plan to publish the new textbook next month.

vocabulary.meaning: to give a spoken or written account of something; or a detailed description of an event
vocabulary.example: She was asked to report on the progress of the project. / The school report shows his grades are improving.

vocabulary.meaning: to ask for something politely; or a polite demand or wish
vocabulary.example: He requested a glass of water. / The DJ played a song by request.

vocabulary.meaning: to make a clear sound like a bell, or to phone someone
vocabulary.example: I heard the front doorbell ring. / I will ring you when I arrive at the station.

vocabulary.meaning: an electrical impulse or radio wave transmitted or received; or a gesture giving information
vocabulary.example: The phone signal is very weak inside this building.

vocabulary.meaning: to make a solemn promise; or to use offensive language
vocabulary.example: I swear to tell the truth. / It is not polite to swear.

vocabulary.meaning: to write text using a keyboard
vocabulary.example: She can type very quickly without looking at the keyboard.

vocabulary.meaning: a person who watches a television program, video, or movie
vocabulary.example: Millions of viewers watched the live broadcast of the game.

vocabulary.meaning: a set of pages on the internet containing information
vocabulary.example: You can find more details on our official website.

vocabulary.meaning: to speak very quietly using one's breath; or a low, quiet sound
vocabulary.example: She whispered the secret in my ear. / They spoke in whispers so as not to wake the baby.

vocabulary.meaning: a member of your family, such as an aunt, uncle, or cousin
vocabulary.example: All of my relatives came to my grandparents' anniversary party.

vocabulary.meaning: an underground railway system; metro
vocabulary.example: The quickest way to travel around London is by the underground.

vocabulary.meaning: a reason that you give to explain why you did something wrong or didn't do something
vocabulary.example: He made a weak excuse for being late to class.

vocabulary.meaning: a person who is active in party politics or government
vocabulary.example: The politician gave a speech about improving public transportation.

vocabulary.meaning: an exciting and unusual, sometimes risky, experience or journey
vocabulary.example: Traveling alone in the mountains was a great adventure.

vocabulary.meaning: a person who translates speech orally from one language into another
vocabulary.example: She worked as an interpreter at the international conference.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to phone someone again or in return
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: I'm busy right now, so I'll call you back later.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to be published or made available to the public
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: When does the new magazine come out?

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to stop the supply of something, or disconnect a phone call
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Their electricity was cut off because they hadn't paid the bill.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to complete a form by writing in the details
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Please fill in this application form and return it to us.
EX: Don't hang up yet, I have one more thing to say.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to stop using a computer system or website
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Remember to log off when you are finished using the computer.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to connect to a computer system or website
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: You need to log on to the school website to check your grades.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to produce a printed copy of a document from a computer
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Could you print out this report for me?

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: by using email, phone, or letter
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: You can contact customer service by e-mail or phone.
EX: I found a lot of useful information on the Internet.
EX: I heard about the traffic jam on the news this morning.
EX: My mother has been on the phone for an hour.
EX: They played my favorite song on the radio.
EX: Is there anything interesting on TV tonight?

wordFormation.meaning: certain: sure or definite / certainly: without doubt, definitely / certainty: the state of being completely confident or sure about something
wordFormation.example:
EX: I am certain that we met before.
EX: She will certainly pass the exam.
EX: There is no certainty that it will rain tomorrow.

wordFormation.meaning: communicate: share or exchange information / communication: the act of sharing or exchanging information
wordFormation.example:
EX: We communicate with our clients by email.
EX: Good communication is key to a successful relationship.

wordFormation.meaning: connect: join two or more things together / connection: a link or relationship / disconnect: break the connection or turn off a device
wordFormation.example:
EX: You can connect your phone to the speaker via Bluetooth.
EX: There is a strong connection between health and diet.
EX: Please disconnect the charger when the battery is full.

wordFormation.meaning: deliver: bring goods or letters to a destination / delivery: the act of bringing goods or letters to a destination
wordFormation.example:
EX: They deliver groceries to your door.
EX: We offer free delivery on orders over fifty dollars.

wordFormation.meaning: express: show a feeling or opinion by words or actions / expression: the act of showing a feeling or look on one's face / expressive: showing feelings or thoughts clearly
wordFormation.example:
EX: She found it difficult to express her feelings.
EX: He had a sad expression on his face.
EX: She has very expressive eyes.

wordFormation.meaning: inform: tell someone about facts or information / informative: providing useful or interesting information / information: facts provided or learned about something
wordFormation.example:
EX: Please inform us of any changes to your schedule.
EX: The lecture about history was very informative.
EX: You can get more information at the reception desk.

wordFormation.meaning: predict: say that an event will happen in the future / prediction: a statement about what will happen / predictable or unpredictable: behaving in a way that is expected or not expected
wordFormation.example:
EX: It is hard to predict the weather here.
EX: His prediction about the match score was correct.
EX: The ending of the movie was completely predictable.

wordFormation.meaning: secretly: in a private or hidden way / secrecy: the state of being kept secret
wordFormation.example:
EX: They secretly planned a surprise party for him.
EX: The government operates in complete secrecy on this matter.

wordFormation.meaning: speak-spoke-spoken: talk or use voice / speaker: a person who speaks / speech: a formal talk given to an audience
wordFormation.example:
EX: She spoke to the manager about the problem.
EX: The guest speaker gave an interesting talk.
EX: The president delivered a long speech.

wordFormation.meaning: translate: change words from one language into another / translation: translated text or process of translating / translator: a person who translates written text
wordFormation.example:
EX: Can you translate this letter into English?
EX: The book has been published in English translation.
EX: She works as a French-to-English translator.

wordPatterns.meaning: to give an opinion about something
wordPatterns.example: EX: She declined to comment on the rumors.

wordPatterns.meaning: to exchange information or talk with someone
wordPatterns.example: EX: She communicates with her cousins using social media.

wordPatterns.meaning: to look at someone or something very quickly
wordPatterns.example: EX: He glanced at his watch and realized he was late.
EX: I received a lovely postcard from my friend in Italy.

wordPatterns.meaning: to answer someone or a message
wordPatterns.example: EX: Please reply to this email as soon as possible.
EX: I will send the files to you tonight.

wordPatterns.meaning: to speak to someone about a specific subject
wordPatterns.example: EX: We need to talk to the teacher about the test.
EX: Did you tell her about the change in plans?

wordPatterns.meaning: to change words from one language to another
wordPatterns.example: EX: He translated the document from German into Spanish.

wordPatterns.meaning: to write a message to someone regarding a topic
wordPatterns.example: EX: I wrote to my grandparents about my school grades.

wordPatterns.meaning: facts or data regarding a subject
wordPatterns.example: EX: We need more information about the hotel locations.

wordPatterns.meaning: a written message from someone discussing a topic
wordPatterns.example: EX: She received a letter from the bank about her account.
